data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-29T01:38:24.532Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core's Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically regarding synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a knowledge question about .NET migration changes. Let me answer based on what I know about the EF Core Cosmos DB provider's evolution.

In EF Core 9 (with .NET 9), the Cosmos DB provider dropped support for synchronous APIs. Methods like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, `SaveChanges()` etc. on the Cosmos provider started throwing `InvalidOperationException` by default because the underlying Cosmos SDK is async-only.


In .NET 10, there was an opt-in mechanism to enable sync-over-async behavior, which the user configured to work around this limitation. The breaking change in .NET 11 is likely the removal of this opt-in entirely, forcing all Cosmos DB operations to be fully asynchronous with no synchronous fallback available.
